title: Independence Day Address from the Red Fort
titleOriginal: लाल किले से स्वतंत्रता दिवस का संबोधन
sourceLabel: Prime Minister of India Official Website

paragraphs.text: My dear countrymen, I offer my heartfelt greetings to all of you on this auspicious occasion of our Independence Day. Today, from the ramparts of this historic Red Fort, I salute every citizen of India. I salute the brave freedom fighters who sacrificed everything for our independence. Their dream was of a prosperous, self-reliant, and powerful India.

This is the land of 140 crore aspirations. 140 crore dreams. And today, India is not merely dreaming but delivering. India has demonstrated to the world that when a nation of this scale moves with determination and unity, nothing is impossible. Our Chandrayaan mission reached the South Pole of the Moon, and the entire world celebrated with us.

Friends, when I look at India today, I see a nation transformed. Digital payments have reached every corner of this country. Today, India leads the world in real-time digital transactions. A tea seller, a vegetable vendor, a rickshaw driver, all are conducting business through UPI. This is not just a technology achievement. This is a revolution of inclusion.

We spoke of Amrit Kaal, the next 25 years that will shape India's destiny as we march toward the centenary of our independence in 2047. In this Amrit Kaal, every Indian must pledge to work toward a developed India. Not a developing India, my friends, but a developed India. This is our collective resolve.

Infrastructure is the backbone of a developed nation. Today, India is building roads, railways, airports, and ports at a pace never seen before. The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project, new expressways spanning thousands of kilometers, the expansion of our metro networks, waterways development. This is the India of the twenty-first century. An India that is building, not waiting.

Let me speak about our women. The strength of India lies in its women. Today, more women are entering the workforce, starting businesses, leading in science and technology. The Ujjwala scheme has freed millions of women from the drudgery of cooking with firewood. The Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao campaign has changed the way our society thinks about our daughters.

My dear brothers and sisters, corruption was once considered an inevitable part of Indian governance. People had accepted it as their fate. But we said no. Through Direct Benefit Transfer, we have ensured that government money reaches the intended beneficiary directly, without middlemen. Over 30 lakh crore rupees have been transferred directly. This is the new India, where every rupee reaches where it is meant to go.

The world is experiencing a technological revolution, and India must not be a consumer of technology but a creator. In artificial intelligence, quantum computing, semiconductor manufacturing, and space technology, India must lead. I am pleased to announce that our semiconductor mission is progressing rapidly. Global companies are choosing India as their manufacturing base.

Friends, India's G20 presidency this year has been historic. We chose the theme 'One Earth, One Family, One Future.' This is not just a slogan. This is an expression of India's civilizational philosophy. Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam, the world is one family. India brought the voice of the Global South to the table of global governance.

Make in India is no longer just a campaign. It is a movement. From mobile phones to defense equipment, from pharmaceuticals to automobiles, India is manufacturing for itself and for the world. When the world needed vaccines during the pandemic, India stepped up. We produced and distributed vaccines not only for our people but for the entire world.

Our armed forces are the guardians of our sovereignty. I salute every soldier, every officer, every member of our armed forces and paramilitary forces who stands at the border protecting us day and night. India's defense capabilities have grown enormously. We are now among the top five defense spenders in the world, and importantly, we are building indigenous defense capabilities.

Environment and development must go hand in hand. India has committed to achieving net zero by 2070. We are rapidly expanding our renewable energy capacity. Solar energy, wind energy, green hydrogen. The International Solar Alliance, proposed by India, now has over 100 member countries. India is showing the world that a developing nation can grow while protecting its environment.

Education is the foundation of national development. The National Education Policy is transforming our education system. Education in the mother tongue, a focus on practical skills, the integration of technology in classrooms. We are preparing our youth not just for jobs, but for the challenges of the future. Our IITs, our IIMs, our new institutions of eminence are producing world-class talent.

I want to speak directly to the youth of India. You are 65 percent of this nation. The twenty-first century belongs to you. Do not think small. Dream big. Start businesses. Innovate. Create. The startup ecosystem in India has produced over 100 unicorns. This was unimaginable just a decade ago. But you have made it possible, and you will continue to achieve even greater things.

My dear countrymen, as we stand here today, let us resolve together. Let us resolve that we will make India a developed nation by 2047. Let us resolve that no citizen will be left behind. Let us resolve that India will be a leader in science, technology, innovation, and compassion. This is our pledge to the freedom fighters who gave us this nation.

From the ramparts of this Red Fort, I call upon every Indian, every family, every village, every city: come together for the nation. Together, we will build the India of our dreams. Jai Hind! Jai Hind! Jai Hind! Vande Mataram!
